examples.title: fork() System Call Example
examples.description: Demonstrates fork() creating a child process and distinguishing parent from child
examples.explanation: fork() returns twice - once in parent (with child's PID) and once in child (with 0). Parent uses wait() to wait for child completion. Both processes execute the same code after fork().
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(n) where n is process count for scheduling
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) copy-on-write of parent address space

examples.title: File Operations - open, read, write, close
examples.description: Demonstrates basic file management system calls
examples.explanation: open() creates/opens a file and returns file descriptor. write() writes data, read() reads data, lseek() repositions the file pointer, and close() closes the file descriptor.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is bytes transferred, O(1) for open/close/lseek
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for file descriptor, O(n) buffer for read/write

examples.title: Parent-Child Communication using pipe()
examples.description: Demonstrates inter-process communication using pipe system call
examples.explanation: pipe() creates a unidirectional channel. Parent writes to pipefd[1], child reads from pipefd[0]. Both ends must be closed appropriately - parent closes read end, child closes write end.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is message size, blocking until data available
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) buffer for message, O(1) for pipe file descriptors

examples.title: fork() followed by exec() - Process Replacement
examples.description: Demonstrates the common pattern of fork() + exec() to run a new program
examples.explanation: fork() creates child process, then execlp() replaces child's program with 'ls -l'. execlp() only returns on error. Parent uses waitpid() to get child's exit status.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(m+n) for exec where m=path search, n=program loading, O(p) for wait
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for new program image loaded into child address space

examples.title: Signal Handling using signal() System Call
examples.description: Demonstrates handling asynchronous events using signal system call
examples.explanation: signal() associates a handler function with a signal. When the signal arrives (e.g., SIGINT from Ctrl+C), the kernel interrupts normal execution and calls the handler. This allows graceful handling of asynchronous events.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for signal registration and handling overhead
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for signal handler function pointer
